current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: The Etowah River is currently tough for trout fishing due to very warm water temperatures. Recent stocking improves chances with stocked-trout attractor flies and egg patterns.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning is the best window because cooler morning temps and moderate sky cover provide slightly better conditions for locating trout in cold-water refuges and runs.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temp is 80.6°F, flow is 97.7 cfs, gage height is 3.69 ft, and turbidity is 7.1 NTU, indicating warm and mildly stained water.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 20%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with unknown trend, wind from the west at 4.7 mph with gusts to 16.1 mph, and no rain expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek colder, oxygenated water and hold near runs, seams, and structure to avoid stress from warm temperatures. Present smaller natural patterns with clean drifts and use stocked-trout attractor flies like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracells. Exercise caution due to increased trout stress from warm water.
